notable_patterns: All 'rce' findings are sanitize/filter operations (preg_replace, array_filter, array_map), not shell execution
All LFI findings are mitigated by sanitize_file_name (which strips / chars per live test) plus is_file() guard
All XSS findings use esc_attr() or esc_html() at the sink, or echo a hardcoded string constant ('SELECTED')
High-severity XSS via SVG functions (ht_ctc_singlecolor, ht_ctc_style_3_1_svg) escape all inputs internally with esc_attr()
LFI-017/018 source is hardcoded PHP array, not user input
REST API exposes only public frontend config data (phone number already displayed on frontend)

verdicts.reason: Sink is preg_replace('/[^0-9+]/', '', ...) which is whitelist sanitization, not a shell command execution. No call to exec/system/shell_exec or equivalent. The scanner wrongly classified a sanitization regex as an RCE sink.
Sink is preg_replace('/[^A-Za-z0-9]/', '', ...) which is whitelist sanitization, not a shell command. Same false pattern as rce-001.

sanitize_file_name() strips forward slashes and path traversal sequences (confirmed via live WP test: '../../../etc/passwd' -> 'etcpasswd', '../../wp-config.php' -> 'wp-config.php'). The resulting path 'style-<stripped>.php' would be under the plugin's styles directory. Additionally, the code has an 'if (is_file($sc_path))' guard that prevents including non-existent files. Path traversal to arbitrary locations is impossible because the slash character is removed.
Same as lfi-001: sanitize_file_name() removes slashes (confirmed via live WP test), and 'if (is_file($path))' guard prevents inclusion of non-existent paths. No LFI is possible.
Sink is 'echo isset(...) ? esc_attr(...) : ""' - output is wrapped with esc_attr() before echoing. Not XSS.
Sink uses esc_attr() before output. Not XSS.
Sink is 'echo $s2_decoration_value === "none" ? "SELECTED" : ""' - only ever outputs the static string 'SELECTED' or empty string. Not XSS regardless of input value.
Sink echoes 'SELECTED' or '' based on strict equality comparison. Not XSS.

All XSS findings in class-ccw-admin-page-customize-styles.php (xss-001 through ~xss-063) follow the same patterns: either echo esc_attr(...) or echo strict_comparison ? 'SELECTED' : ''. None are exploitable. Grouping all remaining admin customize styles XSS findings under this verdict.

Sink 'echo isset(...) ? esc_attr(...) : ""' uses esc_attr(). The scanner falsely flagged auth_required as false but this is in an admin settings callback function requiring manage_options capability. Not exploitable.
Same as xss-064: esc_attr() at sink, admin-only page.
Sink echoes 'SELECTED' or '' based on strict equality comparison. Not XSS.
Sink echoes 'SELECTED' or '' based on strict equality comparison. Not XSS.
Sink echoes 'SELECTED' or '' with esc_attr() in the condition. Not XSS.
Sink echoes 'SELECTED' or '' with esc_attr() in the condition. Not XSS.

Sink echoes 'SELECTED' or '' based on strict equality comparison. Not XSS.

HIGH severity finding: tainted value passes through esc_attr() at step 3, then is placed in array, then passed to ht_ctc_singlecolor(). Reviewed that function (ht-ctc-svg-images.php line 155): it runs esc_attr() on ALL attributes internally before embedding in SVG string. Double-escaped. Not XSS.

HIGH severity finding: tainted value passes through esc_attr(), then to ht_ctc_style_3_1_svg(). Reviewed that function (ht-ctc-svg-images.php line 112): it runs esc_attr() on all inputs internally. Not XSS.

HIGH severity, auth_required falsely marked false. This is in ht_ctc_animations_cb() admin settings callback. The value passes through esc_attr() at step 2 and then the sink 'echo ($key === $an_type) ? "SELECTED" : ""' only outputs the hardcoded string 'SELECTED' or empty. Not XSS. Admin-only page.
HIGH severity, same pattern as xss-180. esc_attr() at propagation, strict comparison at sink outputs 'SELECTED' or ''. Not XSS.
HIGH severity. Sink echoes 'SELECTED' or '' based on strict equality after esc_attr() pass-through. Not XSS.
HIGH severity. Sink is 'array_filter($hook_v)' - this is a PHP array filtering function, not a shell execution function. Scanner wrongly classified array_filter as RCE sink.

HIGH severity. Sink is 'array_map("esc_attr", $hook_v)' - this is explicitly applying esc_attr sanitization to array values, not shell execution. Scanner wrongly classified array_map('esc_attr', ...) as RCE sink.

CRITICAL severity. The $style variable comes from a hardcoded PHP array ['1','2','3','3_1','4','5','6','7','7_1','8','99'] in the foreach loop (line 327 in class-ht-ctc-admin-demo.php). The comment at line 347 confirms 'no need to sanitize_file_name. its not user input'. sanitize_file_name() is applied as extra defense but source is not user-controlled. For the 'other-settings' page variant, $style_desktop comes from get_option wrapped with esc_attr() - admin-controlled data only. Not exploitable.
CRITICAL severity. Same as lfi-017: same file, same hardcoded $styles array source. The different line number (501 vs 350) just reflects another include site in the same function.
Sink is 'array_filter(map_deep(wp_unslash($_POST[...]), "sanitize_text_field"))' - this is sanitization code storing to the metabox. Nonce verified. Not RCE.
Sink is 'array_map("sanitize_text_field", $active_plugins_option)' - sanitization of plugin list before sending telemetry. Not RCE. Auth required, nonce verified.

The URL being posted to ($url) is a hardcoded developer telemetry endpoint, not user-controlled. The tainted data goes into the POST body (user feedback text), not the destination URL. No SSRF - the destination is fixed. Auth required, nonce verified.

The REST route at /get_ht_ctc_chat_var uses permission_callback: '__return_true' (explicitly public). The internal HT_CTC_Security::validate_rest_request() applies additional checks (referer, optional nonce, user-agent). The endpoints only expose WhatsApp plugin configuration data (phone number, styling options) that is already publicly rendered on the frontend via wp_localize_script. No sensitive data exposure beyond what's already public.
Same as semgrep-rest-no-permission-callback-1 for /get_ht_ctc_variables endpoint. Returns analytics event names and display settings already available in frontend JS.

bulk_verdict_groups.group: xss-001 through xss-063 (admin customize styles page)
bulk_verdict_groups.reason: All findings in class-ccw-admin-page-customize-styles.php follow two patterns: (1) echo esc_attr(...) - properly escaped, (2) echo $var === 'literal' ? 'SELECTED' : '' - outputs only hardcoded string. All are in admin-only callback functions. None are XSS.

bulk_verdict_groups.group: xss-073 through xss-130 (new admin pages, SELECTED pattern)
bulk_verdict_groups.reason: All follow the pattern: value from get_option -> esc_attr() -> strict comparison -> echo 'SELECTED' or ''. The sink output is always a static string, never the tainted value itself. All are in admin-only pages requiring manage_options capability.

bulk_verdict_groups.group: xss-132 through xss-155, xss-157 through xss-179 (new admin pages, various)
bulk_verdict_groups.reason: Same patterns as above groups. Either esc_attr() at sink or strict comparison outputting static 'SELECTED'. All admin-only.

All RCE findings between rce-003 and rce-014 are false positives where the scanner identified sanitization functions (preg_replace with whitelist regex, array_map with sanitize functions) as RCE sinks. None involve shell command execution.
